title: Solve Problems with Unit Rates
contentObjective: I can solve real-world problems by using unit rates to compare options.
languageObjective: I can explain my comparison using the words unit rate, better buy, per unit, and rate.

turnAndTalk.question: As supply manager you compare token deals like 500 for $60 and 350 for $38.50. You can't compare totals directly, so what does finding the cost 'per unit' let you do?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Finding the cost per one token lets me compare deals fairly even when the package sizes differ.

stems.en: I can't compare the totals because ___.
stems.es: No puedo comparar los totales porque ___.

stems.en: Finding the cost ___ lets me compare fairly.
stems.es: Hallar el costo ___ me deja comparar de forma justa.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain the packages have different quantities, so the per-unit cost (unit rate) is needed to compare deals fairly.
turnAndTalk.extend: Does the biggest package always have the lowest unit rate? Predict, then explain how you could be tricked by package size alone.
extendStems: The biggest package is not always cheapest per unit because ___.
Package size can trick you when ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How did the unit rate help you decide the better buy between the two token suppliers in your table?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I found the cost per token for each supplier and chose the lower one.

stems.en: The unit rate for ___ is $___ per token.
stems.es: La tasa unitaria de ___ es $___ por ficha.

stems.en: The better buy is ___ because its unit rate is ___.
stems.es: La mejor compra es ___ porque su tasa unitaria es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students divide price by quantity for each deal (e.g., 350 for $38.50 = $0.11 each) and choose the supplier with the lower unit rate as the better buy.
turnAndTalk.extend: One supplier had the lower price per token but a higher total. Explain why a lower unit rate, not a lower total, is what makes a deal the better buy.
extendStems: A lower total does not mean a better buy because ___.
The unit rate is what matters because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Printer A makes 150 tickets in 5 minutes and Printer B makes 200 in 8 minutes. The manager needs 600 tickets fast. How do unit rates decide the faster printer and the time needed?
turnAndTalk.kernel: I find tickets per minute for each printer, pick the higher rate, then divide 600 by it.

stems.en: Printer A prints ___ tickets per minute and Printer B prints ___.
stems.es: La impresora A imprime ___ boletos por minuto y la B imprime ___.

stems.en: The manager should use Printer ___; 600 tickets take ___ minutes.
stems.es: El gerente debe usar la impresora ___; 600 boletos tardan ___ minutos.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students find 30 and 25 tickets/min, choose Printer A (higher rate), and divide 600 / 30 = 20 minutes.
turnAndTalk.extend: Here the higher unit rate is better, but for the token deals the LOWER unit rate was better. Explain why 'better' depends on what the rate measures.
extendStems: A higher rate is better when ___, but a lower rate is better when ___.
It depends on whether the rate measures ___ or ___.

vocabulary.definition: A rate for just 1 of something, like cost for 1 item.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una tasa para solo 1 de algo, como el precio de 1 artículo.
vocabulary.visual: $2.50 per pound means $2.50 for every 1 pound
vocabulary.cloze: A rate that gives the amount for exactly one unit is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The choice that costs less for each item.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La opción que cuesta menos por cada artículo.
vocabulary.visual: $0.10 each vs $0.15 each — the $0.10 option is the better buy
vocabulary.cloze: The option that costs less per item, found by comparing unit rates, is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: Looking at numbers to see which is bigger or smaller.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Mirar números para ver cuál es mayor o menor.
vocabulary.cloze: Showing how two amounts relate to each other is a ___.

vocabulary.visual: $0.50 per token means each token costs $0.50
vocabulary.cloze: The cost or amount for a single item is the cost ___.

vocabulary.definition: A ratio comparing two amounts with different units, like miles per hour.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una razón que compara dos cantidades con unidades distintas, como millas por hora.
vocabulary.visual: 120 miles in 3 hours is a rate (miles and hours are different units)
vocabulary.cloze: A ratio comparing two quantities with different units is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A math sentence saying two ratios are equal. It helps find a missing number.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una oración matemática que dice que dos razones son iguales. Ayuda a hallar un número que falta.
vocabulary.cloze: An equation stating that two ratios are equal is a ___.

launch.narrative: You're the new supply manager for the arcade! You need to order tokens, prizes, and snacks from different suppliers. Each supplier offers a different package deal. Your job is to find the unit rate for each deal and figure out which supplier gives you the best value for every item you need to order.
launch.contextImage: Arcade supply room with boxes from different vendors showing bulk pricing labels
noticePrompts: How can you compare deals when the quantities are different?
What operation helps you find the cost for just one item?
wonderPrompts: Does the biggest package always give the best unit rate?
How can unit rates help you make smart spending decisions?

explore.instructions: Calculate the unit rate for each supplier's deal. Then determine which is the better buy for each item.

discourse.prompt: How do you use unit rates to determine the better buy?
discourse.sentenceFrame: I find the better buy by comparing the ___ for each option. The option with the ___ unit rate is the better buy because ___.

optional.stem: Apples cost $3 for 2 pounds. What is the price per pound?
A printer prints 90 pages in 6 minutes. How many pages per minute?
At 55 miles per hour, how far do you travel in 4 hours?
A worker earns $54 for 6 hours. At the same rate, how much for 9 hours?

approaching.stem: Which is the better buy: 6 bouncy balls for $4.50 or 10 bouncy balls for $8.00?
choices: They cost the same per ball

approaching.stem: A faucet drips 15 ounces of water in 5 minutes. What is the unit rate?
A printer prints 42 pages in 6 minutes. How many pages does it print in 15 minutes at the same rate?
approaching.explanation: Unit rate: 42 ÷ 6 = 7 pages per minute. In 15 minutes: 7 × 15 = 105 pages.

approaching.stem: Store A sells 5 notebooks for $8. Store B sells 3 notebooks for $5.25. Which has the lower price per notebook?
approaching.explanation: Store A: $8 ÷ 5 = $1.60. Store B: $5.25 ÷ 3 = $1.75. $1.60 < $1.75, so Store A is cheaper per notebook.

approaching.instructions: Find the unit rate for each deal to determine which is cheaper.

onLevel.instructions: Find the unit rate for each deal and identify the better buy.

onLevel.stem: A machine dispenses 24 tokens in 3 minutes. At this rate, how many tokens does it dispense in 10 minutes?
onLevel.explanation: Unit rate: 24 ÷ 3 = 8 tokens per minute. In 10 minutes: 8 × 10 = 80 tokens.

onLevel.instructions: Three friends each walked different distances in different times. Sort them from fastest to slowest.

onLevel.stem: A factory produces 360 widgets in 8 hours. At the same rate, how many widgets are produced in a 12-hour shift?
onLevel.explanation: Unit rate: 360 ÷ 8 = 45 widgets per hour. In 12 hours: 45 × 12 = 540 widgets.

extending.title: Find the Unit Rate Error

workedExample.label: Which is the better buy: 5 lb for $8.75 or 3 lb for $5.10?
Compare
workedExample.work: 0.59 > 0.57, so Deal 2 is the better buy

extending.correctWork: To find cost per pound, divide price by pounds: $8.75 ÷ 5 = $1.75 per lb, $5.10 ÷ 3 = $1.70 per lb. Deal 2 ($1.70/lb) is still the better buy — but the correct unit rates are dollars per pound, not pounds per dollar.
hints: The student divided quantity by price instead of price by quantity.
For 'cost per pound,' divide dollars by pounds: $8.75 ÷ 5 = $1.75/lb.

extending.prompt: Two arcade supply companies offer token deals. Company X: 300 tokens for $36. Company Y: 500 tokens for $55. The arcade needs 1,000 tokens. Find the unit rate for each company, determine which is cheaper per token, and calculate the total cost of buying 1,000 tokens from the better deal.
extending.sentenceFrame: Company X charges $___ per token and Company Y charges $___ per token. The better deal is Company ___ because ___. Buying 1,000 tokens would cost $___.

extending.instructions: Two delivery drones fly at different rates. Drone A travels 9 miles in 12 minutes. Drone B travels 7 miles in 10 minutes. Complete the table and determine which drone delivers faster.

connect.scenario: The arcade needs 600 prize tickets printed. Printer A prints 150 tickets in 5 minutes. Printer B prints 200 tickets in 8 minutes. The manager wants to use the faster printer.
connect.promptQuestion: Which printer should the manager choose, and how long will it take to print all 600 tickets?
connect.prompt: Printer A prints ___ tickets per minute. Printer B prints ___ tickets per minute. The manager should use Printer ___ because it is faster. It will take ___ minutes to print 600 tickets.

exitTicket.stem: A car travels 195 miles on 6 gallons of gas. What is the unit rate in miles per gallon?
